examples.title: Dropping the repeated type name
examples.description: When the type is already declared on the left-hand side, `new()` removes the redundant repetition from fields, locals, and parameter defaults.

examples.title: new() inside collections and constructor calls
examples.description: Collection initializers and method arguments already carry the element type, so `new()` keeps the signal-to-noise ratio high where it matters most.

relatedFeatures.reason: Start with Records when you want an earlier building block for Target-typed new.
Continue to Primary constructors for the next guide in this theme.
